title: Sound Design in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Sound Design in Evangelion             Quote Post by alexprevity » Sat Dec 01, 2012 1:44 pm Hey all! Alex here (yes, I'm new). I am currently studying Sound Design at college, and I had a few questions pertaining to the sound design of Evangelion (series, movies, whatever). Are there any known bonus features, interviews, etc. that explain the postproduction sound process? I know that post sound is kind of and elusive topic in anime and Japanese video games, and it would be cool to get some insight on the process.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Dec 01, 2012 2:12 pm I'm not sure if the original series/movies were too big on DVD special features, and even still sound design is pretty specific. I think your best bet to find anything is to look into the Rebuild movies for that stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by alexprevity » Sat Dec 01, 2012 2:18 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I'm not sure if the original series/movies were too big on DVD special features, and even still sound design is pretty specific. I think your best bet to find anything is to look into the Rebuild movies for that stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:32 am The booklet for the DTS Collector's Edition of the movies ( KIBA-1209 ) had something about the mixing process from the original stereo to the DVD's 5.1 audio, but it's all in Japanese obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by jimmiel » Sun Dec 02, 2012 10:57 am Obviously the greatest source of quality Evangelion discussion in general - the Manga Entertainment commentary for the original Evangelion movies. Among what I'm sure was fascinating, in-depth technical discussion, I recall specifically Amanda Winn-Lee praising the English dub's sound design for its ability to make your "feel the rumble" in your butt from the explosions. Truly illuminating stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Dec 03, 2012 9:58 am Most of what you get in the "sound design" process of a TV series like NGE is really simply "sound mixing," as a lot of it is just sound effects pulled from a pre-existing sound library and used in the final mix of Neon Genesis Evangelion . (Giant footsteps of the Evas, explosions, gun/weapon blasts, ect.) Much of the human character based sound effects are just simply foley. (I'm really not sure if they had the Japanese actors blow in a tube attached to a giant water cooler for the entry plug bubbles, but that's what it sounds like to me.) There was some actual design for the Eva sounds themselves, but it all still seems rather low budget and done very quickly. They would record someone screaming and slow the frequency down to make it sound like it was supposed to emanate from a giant creature. Sachiel had some screeching before self imploding that I'm not sure how they were created. (I forget if that was only in the New Movies or not.) The Angel Ramiel has a choral sound around it, and I'm not sure if that's a stock sound from a library or if Shiro Sagisu (music composer) was responsible for recording a choir. (I doubt the latter explanation though, as I don't think Sagisu had a budget for choirs until The End of Evangelion .) And Pen Pen's is literally the voice-actress for Rei pretending to be a bird. Beyond that, you aren't going to get into a lot of elaborate sound design aspects of Evangelion until you reach the Theatrical Movies, especially the spiffy newer ones with bigger budgets and more flexible production scheduling. (Well, Anno thinks they're flexible anyway.) Most of that stuff would be beyond me (my speciality is visuals and some music, not the nitty-gritty of audio), and I would only be able to tell you the effect it had on my viewing experience instead of the creation process. I would remark that I'm pretty sure it helps that most of the Angels are silent beasts. Unit 01 tends to be the biggest noise-making creature in the series, and she can be voiced by anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Dec 03, 2012 11:26 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: And Pen Pen's is literally the voice-actress for Rei pretending to be a bird.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Dec 03, 2012 11:36 am A.T. Fish wrote: What? Pen Pen just became much more awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Dec 03, 2012 12:14 pm jimmiel wrote: Obviously the greatest source of quality Evangelion discussion in general - the Manga Entertainment commentary for the original Evangelion movies. Among what I'm sure was fascinating, in-depth technical discussion, I recall specifically Amanda Winn-Lee praising the English dub's sound design for its ability to make your "feel the rumble" in your butt from the explosions. Truly illuminating stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Dec 03, 2012 2:06 pm I wonder if placing the Eva sounds in the dialogue mix was just another way of the filmmakers at GAINAX realizing that the Evas and Angels were in fact characters. Who's to say Yui was really all that different being the soul of Unit 01, or Kaworu was really all that different than the other Angels trying to get past Shinji or Yui's A.T. Field? A.T. Fish wrote: What? Pen Pen just became much more awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Mon Dec 03, 2012 3:10 pm Does anyone here know where Evangelion gets its stock sound effects? Most of them are pretty widely used Anime sounds, and they must come from some library, but I've been looking for ages and haven't found anything.

[]:             Quote Post by tootiredtoriot » Mon Dec 03, 2012 6:09 pm The Platinum Collection I've got has a bit of commentary on the remixing. From taking it to surround sound music in the intro, to playing with reverbs etc on the dialogue to emulate the actual environments, eg inside the plug. (without going into the encyclopedia of editions, it looks like this) SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by alexprevity » Mon Dec 03, 2012 6:59 pm tootiredtoriot wrote: The Platinum Collection I've got has a bit of commentary on the remixing. From taking it to surround sound music in the intro, to playing with reverbs etc on the dialogue to emulate the actual environments, eg inside the plug.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Mon Dec 03, 2012 9:20 pm As for Evas in the dialogue mix - might as well use the voice talent if it's there. Hayashibara did the Eva-01 scream. "Audio Tanaka" did the sound production, so they probably had all the stock sounds.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Tue Dec 04, 2012 4:05 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Yes. Apparently Rei Ayanami, Yui Ikari, Faye Valentine, and Paprika are all also Pen Pen, because Megumi Hayashibara can become anything she wants to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 04, 2012 7:42 am A.T. Fish wrote: I wonder if she also did the voice for the alternate universe version of Rei from the last tv episode, I just rewatched it and she sounded very different from Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue Dec 04, 2012 8:22 am You should check out her role as Momoko in Patlabor where she plays a super energetic news reporter. You wouldn't believe that's the same person who voiced Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Tue Dec 04, 2012 9:20 am Fireball wrote: You should check out her role as Momoko in Patlabor where she plays a super energetic news reporter. You wouldn't believe that's the same person who voiced Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Dec 04, 2012 6:25 pm Yeah, characters like Rei were the exception to Hayashibara's usual roles. In fact, she had originally auditioned for the role of Misato in NGE because that's just the kind of character she's used to playing. Anno decided to troll the fans by giving Hayashibara her first quite and subdued role, surprising her fans by only speaking one line in her first NGE episode.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Dec 04, 2012 6:34 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Anno decided to troll the fans by giving Hayashibara her first quite and subdued role, surprising her fans by only speaking one line in her first NGE episode.
